comparable_grant.recipient: CA AQUATIC THERAPY AND WELLNESS CENTER

viability.tier_reason: Application process unclear - contact to verify
red_flags: Extremely limited public information - only accessible through nonprofit databases
No website or published grant guidelines
Specific medical focus (pulmonic conditions) may exclude patient safety work unless directly clinical
Administrative structure through bank suggests formal, institutional giving patterns
No evidence of interest in healthcare quality/safety rather than condition-specific treatment
viability.recommendation: POSSIBLE with CAUTION - HIGHEST PRIORITY OF FAMILY FOUNDATIONS. Foundation has explicit medical field focus which is more aligned with PSMF than most other targets. However, lack of public information, specific pulmonic focus, and unclear grant process present challenges. STRONGLY RECOMMEND: (1) Phone contact through US Bank NA (858-410-0986) to inquire about current grant interests, application process, and whether patient safety/quality improvement fits within foundation scope, (2) Request and review recent 990-PF filings to understand recent grantee patterns and infer true priorities, (3) Assess whether PSMF can articulate specific pulmonary/respiratory patient safety work. Probability 25-40% is highest among family foundations but still uncertain due to limited transparency. This is the only realistic target among the three family foundations.

current_opportunities.name: Medical Field Grants
current_opportunities.description: Foundation has documented focus on medical field with specific interest in pulmonary/pulmonic conditions. Supports schools, colleges, and cultural organizations.
current_opportunities.deadline: NONE (per available records) - Limited to medical field
current_opportunities.focus: Medical field broadly, specifically pulmonic/pulmonary conditions
current_opportunities.eligibility: Medical research and clinical organizations. Patient safety fit uncertain.
current_opportunities.fit_with_psmf: Potential - If foundation has interest in clinical safety within pulmonary/respiratory care

foundation_priorities: Schools and cultural organizations (per IRS activity codes)

red_flags: Very small asset base ($870K) with limited annual giving capacity (~$177K)
No public website or published grant guidelines
No specified funding priorities or focus areas
Minimal online presence suggests either dormancy or very informal operation
Average grant size (~$28K) is significantly below typical institutional nonprofit funding needs
viability.recommendation: POSSIBLE but LIMITED. Small asset base and unclear priorities suggest low-to-moderate probability. Worth investigating further if PSMF has California nonprofit networks or connections that know this family foundation. Direct phone outreach (818-623-9898) to ask about funding interests and application process recommended. Assess fit only if foundation confirms healthcare/safety interest. Not priority target unless connection exists or foundation articulates relevant mission.
